activityBar.dockedHint: (docked — drag to right bar to undock)

canvas.noAnimationDesc: Run code to generate the animation
canvas.noGraphDesc: Submit code to generate the call graph

statusBar.idle: Ready — press Run to start analysis

empty.animationDesc: Run code to generate the animation
empty.graphDesc: Run code to generate the call graph
empty.historyEmptyDesc: Your history will appear here after running code

outro.title: Start Your Algorithm Experiments!
outro.description: You've learned the core features of the Playground. Now write some code, run it, and watch the visualization. Algorithm animations are currently supported for {{algos}}.

codeEditor.title: Code Editor
codeEditor.description: Write Python code in main.py on the left — this is your starting point for experimenting with algorithms.

runButton.title: Run Code
runButton.description: Click "Run" to execute your code. After analysis completes, an algorithm detection dialog may appear — pick the best match to load the animation. Give it a try!

algoDetection.title: Algorithm Detection
algoDetection.description: If a detection dialog appeared, your selection has been applied. If you skipped it or no match was found, you can still explore the call graph — or re-run with a supported algorithm to load the animation.

tabBar.title: Visualization Mode
tabBar.description: After running, switch here between "Algorithm Animation" and "Call Graph / CFG" visualization modes.

canvas.title: Visualization Canvas
canvas.description: This is the main stage, showing how data changes at each step of the algorithm. You can zoom and pan to inspect details.

controlBar.title: Playback Controls
controlBar.description: Use play, pause, previous, and next to control the animation. You can also drag the progress bar to jump to any step or adjust speed.

aiAnalysis.title: AI Analysis
aiAnalysis.description: After execution completes, click here to view the AI algorithm detection and analysis results for your code.

rightPanels.title: Data Panels & Toolbar
rightPanels.description: The right toolbar lets you toggle panels for variables, call stack, Console, and more to inspect runtime state.

dragDock.title: Dock a Panel to the Left
dragDock.description: Grab the first panel icon in the right toolbar (right orange frame) and drag it onto the left bar (left orange frame) to dock the panel beside the editor. Give it a try!

history.title: Execution History
history.description: Click the clock icon in the bottom-left to open execution history and replay previous runs.

run.emptyCode: Please enter some code before running.
run.timeout: Execution timed out — try a smaller input.
run.poolExhausted: Server is busy. Please try again later.
run.truncated: Output may be truncated — trace limit reached.
run.duplicateCode: This code is already in your history.
